file_name: Kim - 2024 - Investigating skin cancer diagnosis using a webcam-based microcontroller system.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper does not use AI, machine learning, or deep learning to obtain results. The study employs a hardware-based prototype (ASCD system using Arduino and ArduCam) with bandpass filters to capture RGB images. The analysis relies on the modified Beer-Lambert law to quantitatively determine skin component levels (melanin, oxyhemoglobin, deoxyhemoglobin) from reflectance values. While OpenCV is mentioned for image processing, it is used for standard image handling rather than AI-based classification or prediction. The results are derived from physical optics and mathematical modeling, not computational learning models.

non_compliance_reason: The paper does not use AI to obtain results in dermatology.
